seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mingoola and Minnie Downs. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mingoola vs Minnie Downs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mingoola and Minnie Downs.
heading: Mingoola vs Minnie Downs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mingoola, Queensland and Minnie Downs, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Minnie Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Mingoola (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mingoola or Minnie Downs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mingoola has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Minnie Downs scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mingoola scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Minnie Downs scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mingoola or Minnie Downs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Minnie Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
